all_violations.description: UUID slices are generated inline for provider correlation IDs, reducing replay determinism.
all_violations.suggestion: Use deterministic token helpers tied to testInfo for replay-friendly identifiers.

all_violations.description: Missed-call path also creates non-deterministic provider IDs.
all_violations.suggestion: Align with deterministic ID utility shared by other ConnectShyft ATDD suites.

all_violations.description: E2E missed inbound payload uses random UUID slices for event IDs.
all_violations.suggestion: Use deterministic test IDs to simplify cross-run debugging and triage.

all_violations.description: Suite enforces serial mode, signaling shared-state coupling and reduced isolation.
all_violations.suggestion: Refactor data setup so cases can execute in parallel safely.

all_violations.description: Static thread IDs are reused across all tests, increasing collision risk across retries/workers.
all_violations.suggestion: Namespace thread IDs per test run or seed isolated records per test case.

all_violations.description: Inbound payload fixture targets shared thread context without explicit teardown.
all_violations.suggestion: Add per-test cleanup/seed reset helper to keep fixture state fully isolated.

all_violations.description: API suite is 248 lines and mixes four behavioral concerns in one file.
all_violations.suggestion: Split into focused case modules (placement, lifecycle, closed-thread fallback).
all_violations.code_snippet: test.describe('Story ux-r3 Voicemail and Indicator Behavior (ATDD API RED)', () => { ... })

all_violations.description: E2E suite is 163 lines and combines multiple flows that can be modularized.
all_violations.suggestion: Split E2E scenarios into smaller files by acceptance criterion.
all_violations.code_snippet: test.describe('Story ux-r3 Voicemail and Indicator Behavior (ATDD E2E RED)', () => { ... })

all_violations.description: Webhook POST setup pattern is repeated with small payload variations.
all_violations.suggestion: Extract helper for inbound webhook invocation and expected envelope assertions.

all_violations.description: Cases include priority tags but no stable story/test IDs in title prefix.
all_violations.suggestion: Adopt stable IDs (e.g., UX-R3-API-001) for traceability in reports.
all_violations.code_snippet: '[P0] voicemail on claimed threads remains in Mine ... @P0'

all_violations.description: ux-r3 suite does not include explicit unauthorized/forbidden access checks for endpoints it exercises.
all_violations.suggestion: Add one negative auth contract case to ensure guardrails remain enforced.

all_violations.description: No dedicated replay/idempotency regression in ux-r3 file (currently delegated to e-3/e-4).
all_violations.suggestion: Keep delegation documented or add a lightweight ux-r3 replay sanity check.

all_violations.description: Serial mode prevents worker-level parallelism for this suite.
all_violations.suggestion: Refactor fixture/state model for parallel-safe execution and remove serial mode.

all_violations.description: Each case performs full UI login and multiple navigations, increasing runtime.
all_violations.suggestion: Consider storage-state/session reuse for ux-r3 E2E flows.

all_violations.description: Multiple before/after detail fetches and webhook calls per case increase execution time.
all_violations.suggestion: Consolidate baseline/detail checks where possible without losing assertion value.

all_violations.description: Suite does not opt into parallel mode and can likely be partially parallelized after data isolation updates.
all_violations.suggestion: Enable parallel execution for independent AC-focused cases once fixtures are isolated.

high_severity_violations.description: Suite enforces serial mode, signaling shared-state coupling and reduced isolation.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Refactor data setup so cases can execute in parallel safely.

high_severity_violations.description: API suite is 248 lines and mixes four behavioral concerns in one file.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Split into focused case modules (placement, lifecycle, closed-thread fallback).
high_severity_violations.code_snippet: test.describe('Story ux-r3 Voicemail and Indicator Behavior (ATDD API RED)', () => { ... })

high_severity_violations.description: E2E suite is 163 lines and combines multiple flows that can be modularized.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Split E2E scenarios into smaller files by acceptance criterion.
high_severity_violations.code_snippet: test.describe('Story ux-r3 Voicemail and Indicator Behavior (ATDD E2E RED)', () => { ... })

high_severity_violations.description: Serial mode prevents worker-level parallelism for this suite.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Refactor fixture/state model for parallel-safe execution and remove serial mode.

top_10_recommendations.recommendation: Eliminate serial-only requirement by isolating thread data per test.
Add teardown/reset utility for ux-r3 fixtures to reduce cross-test coupling.
Run ux-r3 API tests under parallel mode in CI once isolation refactor is complete.
Modularize ux-r3 API and E2E suites by acceptance criterion.
Introduce shared webhook/assertion helpers to cut repetition.
Add stable case IDs to improve CI and defect traceability.
Remove serial execution constraint after isolation refactor.
Reuse authenticated storage state for E2E where feasible.
Reduce repeated webhook setup via shared helper and parameterized cases.
Standardize deterministic providerEventId/providerLegId generation in ux-r3 suites.
